rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROSIA L. HART REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/17/2021. 1) Paragraph 11 of the petition alleges that Henry Gaines is deceased, but no alternate contingent beneficiaries named in the trust amendment are identified. Also, the proof of service does not show notice was given to those beneficiaries. 2) The proof of service and paragraph 11 of the petition show different addresses for Adeline Reed, Jackie Smith, and Andrew Smith. 3) Witherspoon & Siracusa's address listed in the proof of service does not include a suite number set forth in paragraph 12 of the petition. 4) Schedule C shows various utility disbursements, but the first account did not report any. Please explain why. 5) The receipts and disbursements must be allocated between principal and income. CRC 7.901(c). 6) The outstanding balances reported on Schedule H appear to have added interest, even though no compound interest was approved. Also, did the Court approve any interest on the balance of the fees of the petitioner and her attorney? 7) Exhibit A includes the petitioner's services rendered before 03/19/2018, but the order on the first account appears to have already compensated the petitioner for such services. 8) Page 5 is missing from the bank statements for October 2018 and September 2019 on file.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUADALUPE OCHOA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/10/21. (No earlier availability.) The 13100 affidavit(s) have not yet been filed. They must be filed conventionally. [LRSF 14.36 G. and 14.60 E.] The Remittitur noted that Respondents were to recover costs. Why are the administrators requesting to pay them out of the estate? The costs incurred by litigation counsel are not sufficiently described (date, payee, description, amount). The attorney's more complete "certification" about the bank statements is still not verified by her verification of the second amended petition as to that allegation, but as accounting was waived, original statements were no longer required to be filed. Courtesy copies of any responsive papers must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/29/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BROOKSEY M ALLEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if the petition should be granted, if there is no conflict with Counsel's representation of this petitioner after representing the prior petitioner, who is the administrator of the former administrator's estate. 1) Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities has no telephone number. 2) Is a P.O. Box address for the proposed personal representative sufficient? 3) The prior administrator indicated in his initial petition that Decedent was either divorced or never married, but this petition indicates "spouse deceased" in item 5a(2) of her petition. 4) All of the assets are reportedly personal property estimated at $200,000, but only limited IAEA is requested in addition to $220,000 bond. Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTOR CADIZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/12/21. (No earlier availability.) No proof of service of notice of the AMENDED FIRST AND FINAL ACCOUNT, etc., filed on 2/16/21 has been filed yet. A proof of service filed on 3/18/21 is for an "Amended Second and Final Account," etc. No "Amended Second and Final Account," etc., has been filed. An UN-filed copy of a pleading with that caption was received by the Examiner on 3/24/21. The amended first account has not yet been adjudicated; there can be no second account yet, amended or otherwise. The "Amended Second and Final Account," etc., being an UNFILED pleading, will not be reviewed. [14.7 C.] Courtesy copies of the correct proof of service or the correct SECOND AMENDED FIRST AND FINAL ACCOUNT, etc., with proof of service of the SECOND AMENDED FIRST AND FINAL ACCOUNT, etc., or an amendment to the [second] amended first and final account that was filed on 2/16/21 must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/3/21.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/10/2021 to allow a court investigator's report on the related conservatorship to be completed. Neither the note nor the deed of trust were included in the order approving the petition for substituted judgment.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT LEE QUINN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/3/2021. ISSUES: 1) Petitioner was appointed with $1,375,000.00 bond. The I&A filed 01/27/2020 alleges $1,684,449.55 in assets. Supplemental I&A filed 01/21/2021 alleging $7,362.25 in assets. (See LRSF 14.16 A. Duty to Apply for Order Increasing Bond.) If additional bond is not on file 5 court days prior to the next hearing petitioner may face suspension. 2) Court to determine how to proceed. Whether to order a 2-year account pursuant to Probate Code 10950. Petition alleges on page 3 lines 24-25 the estate is in condition to be closed and petitioner is preparing an account. Page 4 lines 16-19 allege a first account cannot be filed until the Supplemental I&A is filed. The Final I&A alleging $1,683,449.55 in assets was filed 01/27/2020. The Supplemental I&A alleging 7362.25 in assets was filed 01/20/2021. Examiner does not see a reason why a petition for final distribution has not been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCES YEE CHIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/12/21. (No earlier availability.) The original bank statement has not yet been filed. [LRSF 14.35 D.] Whenever originals are E-filed, the attorney or the fiduciary who retains the original must file a declaration certifying that the original document(s) will be retained in the same way as documents are retained under Local Rule 2.11 M.4., which provides that the original(s) must be retained "for at least the period after the termination, including all appeals, of the case required for conventionally filed documents." The calculation of the fee base is incorrect. [Probate Code Section 10800(b) and CRC 7.705.] It appears that the refund (the only receipt) was likely for amounts paid before the date of death, but the accounting is not clear. (Refunds for amounts paid during estate administration are not proper receipts that may be included in the fee base.) Schedule E (Disbursements) appears to reflect a duplicate payment of $40 on 11/20/20. Were they two separate charges for messenger and filing? (It would be helpful if the description showed what was filed or sent via messenger to whom, etc.) The executor's address was never set forth in the original Petition for Probate. An address in care of her attorney is not adequate. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution," or "Order Terminating Conservatorship [or Guardianship or Trust]." [LRSF 14.36 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/3/21. If the higher statutory fee will be requested, and the amending pleading cannot be filed in time to give proper notice of the amendment (or amended petition) for the hearing on 5/12/21, Counsel should contact the Examiner to request additional time.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARITSA CALDERON SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The account alleges $680,877.55 cash in a Citisavings Account. The FDIC insurance limit is $250,000.00. At the 02/22/2021 hearing Ms. Steneberg stated she believes the funds have been invested and are diversified and she can file a declaration. The declaration shows as of 02/28/2021 most of the funds have been invested in ETF's. This court requires funds to meet requirements of Probate Code 2574. Petitioner should be prepared to explain on what date petitioner invested the funds in the ETF's. No court approval was sought for ETF's that do not meet the requirements of Probate Code 2574. 2) On schedule C - Disbursements entry 12/05/2019 allege a disbursement for miscellaneous expenses totaling $3,720.37. Petitioner alleges $2,520.06 is for CBD medication to control seizures. The remaining $1,200.31 Petitioner alleges was for gas coffee/tea for a cross country vacation and the trust paid 1/3 of the expenses, Maritsa's portion. However, the receipts provided appear to show the trust paying the entire amount. 3) Petitioner amended the account schedules to begin 2/14/2019 but did not amend the information in the petition to reflect the 2/14/2019 account starting date.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EMMA EDWARDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/17/21. Notice must also be given to the Director of Health Care Services, if the decedent was the surviving spouse of a person who received Medi-Cal benefits. Probate Code Section 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or BENEFICIARY is or has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. The date in paragraph 6 of the petition is incomplete. When was notice mailed to the FTB? The I&A filed on 12/24/20 (not 12/21/20) was not properly verified by either the executor or the attorney and item 5 of the I&A was not answered. A declaration by the executor that he has the original bank statements is not sufficient; he must certify that the original document(s) will be retained in the same way as documents are retained under Local Rule 2.11 M.4. Please file a copy of the seller's closing statement to verify the costs of sale. Receipts that were refunds for amounts paid during estate administration may not be included in the calculation of the fee base. A general pecuniary bequest not made within one year from the date of death bears interest. [Probate Code Sections 12003 and 12001.] An amendment to the petition or a fully amended petition, signed and verified by the executor, must be filed to address the issues above. An endorsed filed copy of the amendment (or fully amended petition) and any other papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/6/21. If the amending pleading cannot be filed in time to give proper notice before the continued hearing date, after the paper has been filed, Counsel should contact the Examiner to request a further continuance. An amending pleading requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] Notice of this amended petition was defective. The proof of service filed on 3/18/21 shows that notice was mailed on 2/26/21, before the amended petition was filed. [LRSF 14.14 A.] An appearance may be required in connection with the executor's hiring and paying his own sons. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution," or "Order Terminating Conservatorship [or Guardianship or Trust]." [LRSF 14.36 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA DONSKOI ALEKSANDER ALEKSAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/3/2021. 1) Objector has not provided examiner with the required courtesy copy. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. 2) The Will is not original. Please provide legal authority and explain why the original Will has not been filed. It appears the petition may need to be amended to a petition for probate of lost will and for letters testamentary. 3) Please explain how the copy of the will is self-proving under Probate Code 8220. 4) Copy of will translated does not conform to LRSF 14.15 D. Whenever an instrument written in a foreign language is offered for probate or attached as an exhibit to a pleading, it must always be accompanied by a copy translated into English. All translations must be accompanied by a declaration setting forth the translator's qualifications and/or credentials. [LRSF 14.15 D.] 5) Must file Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). 6) Must file proof of publication. 7) Must file Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147). 8) Must file confidential statement. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 9) Nikita Volchetvskiy is not listed on page 4 under #8. Please explain the name, relationship to decedent, age, and address. 10) Exhibit C the Declaration of Donskaya Tatiana Antolievna is not signed. Please have declarant sign a declaration under penalty of perjury. 11) Exhibit D the Declaration of Donskaya Tatiana Antolievna is not signed. Please have declarant sign under penalty of perjury. 12) Exhibit E must be translated and have a declaration. Whenever an instrument written in a foreign language is offered for probate or attached as an exhibit to a pleading, it must always be accompanied by a copy translated into English. All translations must be accompanied by a declaration setting forth the translator's qualifications and/or credentials. [LRSF 14.15 D.] 13) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/19/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
